mechanism_narrative: Persistent monthly declines in nonfarm payrolls indicate ongoing economic contraction, reducing corporate earnings expectations and leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days. This signal is always active because payroll change is a continuous measure of labor market health.
failure_condition: The month-over-month change in PAYEMS has zero correlation with 20-day forward S&P 500 returns over any 5-year rolling window, or the rank IC remains below 0.03 over 3 years.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate drops to or below 1.0%, the monetary policy stance is extremely accommodative, compressing discount rates and boosting equity valuations. This threshold condition signals the likely onset of an easing cycle that supports positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: After FEDFUNDS falls to or below 1.0%, the 60-day forward S&P 500 return is not significantly positive (one-sided t-test p > 0.05) over all available historical episodes.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month declines in retail sales and nonfarm payrolls for two consecutive months confirm a synchronized demand shock that severely impairs near-term earnings, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the following 20 trading days. The joint confirmation is necessary to filter out noise.
candidate_trigger: Both RSAFS and PAYEMS decline month-over-month for 2 consecutive months
failure_condition: In instances where both series decline for two months, the 20-day forward S&P 500 return is positive in more than 50% of cases over any 10-year window.

mechanism_narrative: In a high-inflation regime (CPI year-over-year above 4%), a 25-basis-point increase in the federal funds rate tightens financial conditions prematurely, raising discount rates and depressing equity prices. This signal is active only when inflation is elevated and the Fed acts to curb it.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS increases by at least 25bp in one month while CPIAUCSL year-over-year growth exceeds 4%
failure_condition: Following such tightening events under high inflation, the 20-day forward S&P 500 return is positive on average, or the negative return rate is below 60%.

mechanism_narrative: The level of industrial production is mean-reverting; when it is low relative to its long-term trend, future increases are expected, lifting corporate output and earnings. This always-on signal predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days as the economy normalizes.
failure_condition: The level of INDPRO standardized by its 5-year moving average has zero predictive correlation with 60-day forward S&P 500 returns over any 10-year period.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp monthly decline in retail sales (more than 2%) signals an abrupt drop in consumer spending that triggers near-term negative earnings revisions, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days. The short horizon captures immediate market reaction.
failure_condition: In months when RSAFS declines by more than 2%, the subsequent 5-day S&P 500 return is positive in more than 50% of occurrences, or the average return is not significantly negative.

mechanism_narrative: When industrial production falls and the unemployment rate rises simultaneously over three months, the economy is in a confirmed contraction that depresses corporate earnings expectations, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days. The joint deterioration reinforces the signal beyond what each series alone provides.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO declines and UNRATE rises month-over-month for 3 consecutive months
failure_condition: When both conditions hold for three months, the 20-day forward S&P 500 return is positive in more than 40% of cases, or the average return is not significantly negative.

mechanism_narrative: In regimes of extreme labor distress where the unemployment rate exceeds 10%, a further increase signals that the economy is reaching a depression trough, prompting anticipation of aggressive policy intervention and eventual recovery. This contrarian signal predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: When UNRATE exceeds 10%, the subsequent 60-day S&P 500 return is negative on average, or positive more than 50% of the time fails to reach a statistically significant mean difference from zero.
